original: NASA selects 3 part configuration for Space Shuttle
Donald Jackson of Canada is 1st to land a triple lutz ice skate jump
Chatrooms make their debut on ARPANET, the forerunner of the Internet
Jevgeni Kulikov skates world record 500m (37.99 sec)
Sergei Bubka pole vaults world record 6.14m (20 feet 1 3/4)
Announcement of the discovery of 90377 Sedna, the farthest natural object in the Solar system so far observed
Google's DeepMind artificial intelligence wins Go challenge against Lee Se-dol 4-1
WPL Cricket Final, Brabourne Stadium, Mumbai: Mumbai Indians claim 2nd title with 8 run win over Delhi Capitals; Player of the match: Harmanpreet Kaur (MI) 66 (44)
Climate change strikes held by schoolchildren take place around the world, inspired by Swedish teenagerGreta Thunberg
Emil Adolf von Behring, German physician, 1901 Nobel Prize for Physics for his serum to cure diphtheria, born in Hansdorf, Prussia (d. 1917)
Alan Bean, American Naval captain and astronaut (Ap 12, Skylab 3, 4th to walk on the moon), born in Wheeler, Texas (d. 2018)
Chris Sanders, American animator and director (Lilo & Stich; The Croods), born in Colorado Springs
Greatest 24-hr rainfall begins: 187 cm at La Reunion, Indian Ocean
Waldemar Haffkine, Ukrainian bacteriologist (1st to develop and use vaccines against cholera and bubonic plague), born in Odessa, Russian Empire (d. 1930)
NASA reports accelerated breakdown of ozone layer by CFK
Wilt Chamberlainis 1st to score 4,000 pts in an NBA season
LIFE magazine callsJimi Hendrix"most spectacular guitarist in the world"
Anne McCaffreymakes history as the first woman to win a Nebula Award (Best Novella) with her work "Dragonrider"
Ranganandhan Francis, Indian field hockey goalkeeper (Olympic gold 1948, 52, 56), born in Rangoon, Myanmar (d. 1975)
Sly Stone [Sylvester Stewart], American funk-rock singer-songwriter, and musician (Sly & The Family Stone - "Everyday People"; "Dance To The Music"), born in Denton, Texas (d. 2025)[1][2]
Moungi Bawendi, French-Tunisian-American chemist (2023 Nobel Prize for discovery of quantum dots), born in Paris, France
A revolution breaks out in Hungary. The Habsburg rulers are compelled to meet the demands of the Reform party.
Key Largo Coral Reef Preserve, also known as John D. Pennekamp Coral Reef State Park, established as 1st underwater park, off the coast of Florida
As a symbol of wartime baseball, Bert Shepard (one-legged WWII veteran) begins a successful tryout as a pitcher for the Washington Senators
WMFE TV channel 24 in Orlando, FL (PBS) begins broadcasting
Sidath Wettimuny scores Sri Lanka's first Test Cricket century (157) in 2nd Test v Pakistan in Faisalabad
Nicolas Louis de Lacaille, French astronomer who mapped the stars of the Southern Hemisphere, born in Rumigny, France (d. 1762)
Cricket's inaugural Test match commences as Australia plays England at the Melbourne Cricket Ground; Australia wins by 45 runs in 4 days
1st streamlined submarine of US navy, USS Nautilus, launched
NAACP begins coordinated attack on segregation & discrimination
John Lee appointed 1st black commissioned officer in US Navy
Five research groups announce the discovery of antimatter
Pitts Penguins' Joe Mullen, is 1st American to score 500 NHL goals
Largest sandstorm in northern China in a decade engulfs Beijing turning air pollution levels hazardous
Another atmospheric river creates a storm across California, bringing widespread flooding and power outages, with Monterey County issuing a 25 mile evacuation order
Oscar Robertsonof Cincinnati Royals scores a NBA midwest region-record 56-point game
Johann Josef Loschmidt, Austrian scientist who made the first accurate estimate of the size of molecules, born in Carlsbad, Bohemia (d. 1895)
Bert Bolin, Swedish meteorologist (1st Chairman Intergovernmental panel on Climate Change), born in Nyköping, Sweden (d. 2007)
Mike Love, American vocalist (Beach Boys - "Fun, Fun, Fun"; "California Girls"), born in Los Angeles, California
Levan Tediashvili, Georgian freestyle wrestler (Olympic gold USSR 82kg 1972, 90kg 1976; World C'ship gold x 4), born in Sagarejo, Georgia (d. 2024)
David Silverman, American animator (The Simpsons), born on Long Island, New York
NYC hires Dr Wallace E. Howell as its official "rainmaker" to help end a crippling water shortage for the princely sum of $100 a day
Expo '70 opens in Osaka, Japan
Finland is 1st European country to give women the right to vote
The Vessel, a honeycomb viewing structure designed by Thomas Heatherwick opens at Hudson Yards development, New York City
Larry HolmesTKOs David Bey in 10 for heavyweight boxing title

14th Rock and Roll Hall of Fame inductees:Billy Joel;Curtis Mayfield;Paul McCartney; Del Shannon; Dusty Springfield;Bruce Springsteen; The Staple Singers; Charles Brown; Bob Wills and His Texas Playboys; andGeorge Martin
Coenraad Johannes van Houten, Dutch chemist and cocoa manufacturer (Dutch process chocolate), born in Amsterdam, Batavian Republic (d. 1887)
Grace Chisholm Young, British mathematician and first woman to obtain a doctorate in Germany, born in Haslemere, England (d. 1944)[1]
E. Donnall Thomas, American physician (1990 Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine for bone marrow transport), born in Mart, Texas (d. 2012)[1]
Zhores Alferov, Soviet-Russian physicist (2000 Nobel Prize in Physics), born in Vitebsk, Soviet Union (d. 2019)
Charles Lloyd, American jazz and pop saxophonist, arranger, composer (Chico Hamilton,  Cannonball Adderley, The Beach Boys), bandleader, and world music pioneer, born in Memphis, Tennessee
Howard Scott, American rock guitarist, singer, and songwriter (War, 1969-94 - "Cisco Kid"; "Why Can’t We Be Friends?"), born in San Pedro, California
(Ryland) "Ry" Cooder, American Grammy Award-winning blues and world music guitarist and producer (Crossroads; Buena Vista Social Club), born in Los Angeles, California
Jørgen Olsen, Danish singer (The Olsen Brothers) won 2000 Eurovision Song Contest, born in Odense, Denmark
Battle of Guilford Court House; British troops under Cornwallis defeat American forces but their heavy losses led to ceding of territory and a strategic loss
Legendary pitcherCy Youngretires from baseball with 511-315 win-loss record
Louisiana establishes 1st health board to regulate quarantine
Cincinnati Red Stockings become baseball's first professional team with ten salaried players
1st escalator patented by American engineer Jesse Reno - first used at Old Iron Pier, Coney Island, New York City
